add: verb. To join or unite (e.g. one thing to another, or as several particulars) so as to increase the number, augment the quantity, or
addition: noun. The act of adding anything.

ado: noun. Trouble; troublesome business; fuss, commotion.
ain: noun. Alternative form of ayin (“Semitic letter”).
ait: noun. An island in a river, especially the River Thames in England.
and: conjunction. As a coordinating conjunction; expressing two elements to be taken together or in addition to each other.
ani: noun. Any bird of the genus Crotophaga in the cuckoo family.
ant: noun. Any of various insects in the family Formicidae in the order Hymenoptera, typically living in large colonies composed almost entirely
dad: noun. A father, a male parent.
dado: noun. The section of a pedestal above the base.
danio: noun. Any of various fish of the genera Danio or Devario.
din: noun. A loud noise; a cacophony or loud commotion.
dint: noun. Especially in by dint of: force, power.
dit: verb. To stop up; block (an opening); close.
doit: noun. A small Dutch coin, equivalent to one-eighth of a stiver.
don: noun. A university professor, particularly one at Oxford or Cambridge.
idiot: noun. A person of low general intelligence.
inti: noun. The currency of Peru between 1985 and 1991, replacing the sol.
into: preposition. To or towards the inside of.
ion: noun. An atom or group of atoms bearing an electrical charge, such as the sodium and chlorine atoms in a salt solution.
iota: noun. The ninth letter of the Greek alphabet (Ι, ι).
nit: noun. The egg of a louse.
nod: verb. To incline the head up and down, as to indicate agreement.
not: adverb. Negates the meaning of the modified verb.
oat: noun. Widely cultivated cereal grass, typically Avena sativa.
odd: adjective. Differing from what is usual, ordinary or expected.
tad: noun. A small amount; a little bit.
tao: noun. The art or skill of doing something in harmony with the essential nature of the thing.
tin: noun. A malleable, ductile, metallic element, resistant to corrosion, with atomic number 50 and symbol Sn.
toad: noun. An amphibian, a kind of frog (order Anura) with shorter hindlegs and a drier, wartier skin, many in family Bufonidae.

ainoi: noun. lauds; a prayer service following matins
aition: noun. An origin story or myth in Ancient Greece.
dan: noun. A title of honour or respect similar to "master" or "Sir", used of historical and legendary figures of the past.
dand: noun. A kind of push-up in Indian gymnastics.
dao: noun. Any of various traditional Chinese swords with a curved, single-edged blade, primarily used for slashing and chopping.
dation: noun. The act of giving, granting or conferring (e.g. an office) but not liberal as a donation or gift.
diota: noun. A vase or drinking cup with two handles.
doa: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Dom.
doina: noun. A kind of Romanian folk song, often of lament, which has also been adopted into klezmer (Ashkenazi Jewish) music.
donat: noun. A rank in some knightly orders.
ida: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Idakho-Isukha-Tiriki.

itd: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southern Tidung.
naio: noun. Myoporum sandwicense, a flowering plant in the figwort family.
nato: noun. A tree of the genus Mora
nid: noun. Alternative form of nide (“nest of pheasants”).
noa: adjective. Non-taboo; serving to replace a taboo name or term.
oda: noun. A room within a harem.
oni: noun. A Japanese evil spirit or demon.
tai: noun. A fish, the Japanese bream.
toa: noun. A small painted artifact made by the Diyari people of Australia, believed to have been used as place markers or signposts.
